source_docs.title: Microfluidics-free single-cell genomics with templated emulsification

oligos.name: Barcoded bead poly(T) capture oligo (T20 PIPs)
oligos.role: Bead-bound RT / mRNA capture primer carrying cell barcode + UMI + poly(T)

components.name: acrydite_polyT_spacer
components.role: 5' /5Acryd/ acrydite attachment + T spacer to hydrogel

components.name: read1_handle
components.role: Illumina Read 1 primer binding region (contains CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTC)

components.name: cell_barcode
components.role: Cell barcode assembled by split-pool ligation of barcode blocks (~10^8 space)

components.name: polyT_anchor
components.role: poly(dT) capture of mRNA poly(A); V = A/C/G anchor

oligos.notes: Bead synthesis primer from the 2023 paper: /5Acryd/TTTTTTTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACGACTCCTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCC. Split-pool ligation adds four 6-bp barcode blocks + a 12-bp UMI + poly(T) (documented UMI motif NNNNNNNNNNNNTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTV). Effective barcode length of 16 bp and UMI of 12 bp inferred from the analysis parameters used in the paper (CITE-seq Count -cbf 1 -cbl 16 -umif 17 -umil 28); the physical bead is built from combinatorial 6-bp blocks. The exact v4 T20 bead sequence is not printed in the user guide.

oligos.name: TSO (FB0003140)
oligos.notes: Printed as AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAATrGrGrG; 3' rGrGrG folded to GGG. Identical sequence to the prototype PIPS_TSO.

oligos.name: WTA Primer (FB0002006)
oligos.role: Whole-transcriptome amplification primer pair (post-RT cDNA amplification)

components.name: wta_forward
components.role: Forward primer, anneals to bead-side Read 1 handle

components.name: wta_reverse
components.role: Reverse primer, anneals to TSO/ISPCR handle

oligos.notes: PCR amplifies first-strand cDNA flanked by the Read 1 handle (5') and the ISPCR/TSO handle (3').

oligos.name: Library Adapter Mix (FB0001605)
oligos.role: Forked Nextera-style ligation adapter (post-fragmentation A-tailed ligation)

components.name: ligation_adapter
components.role: Top strand; 3' T pairs with A-tail; carries Nextera mosaic-end (ME) TATAAGAGACAG

components.name: ligation_primer
components.role: Bottom strand; 5' /5Phos/; ME reverse complement + P7-primer landing site

oligos.notes: 5' phosphate on the ligation primer. Provides the Read 2 / P7-side handle onto which the Library P7 Index primer anneals.

oligos.name: Library P5 Index (FB0001915-1918, FB0001666-1669)
oligos.notes: 8 i5 indexes provided (501 TAGATCGC, 502 CTCTCTAT, 503 TATCCTCT, 504 AGAGTAGA, 505 GTAAGGAG, 506 ACTGCATA, 507 AAGGAGTA, 508 CTAAGCCT). 3' region reprimes the Read 1 handle.

oligos.name: Library P7 Index (FB0001626-1627, FB0001629-1633, FB0002092)
oligos.notes: 3' end carries the Nextera ME (GTGTATAAGAGACAG) and anneals to the ligated adapter. 8 i7 indexes provided (701 TAAGGCGA, 702 CGTACTAG, 709 GCTACGCT, 704 TCCTGAGC, 705 GGACTCCT, 706 TAGGCATG, 707 CTCTCTAC, 708 CAGAGAGG).

oligos.name: PIPS_TSO (prototype, Supplementary Table 1)
oligos.notes: Printed AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAATrGrGrG; rGrGrG folded to GGG.

oligos.name: PIPS_WTA_primer (prototype)
oligos.notes: Single-primer WTA off the ISPCR handle in the prototype workflow.

oligos.name: PIPS_P5library (prototype Nextera library primer)
oligos.role: Custom P5 library primer for Nextera XT prep (2023 paper prototype)
oligos.notes: Printed with 3' phosphorothioates ...GAGT*A*C. Used with standard Nextera P7 (N70x) indexing primers in the prototype only; the v4 kit uses ligation-based indexing instead.

oligos.name: P5-PE1 (CROP-seq sgRNA enrichment)
oligos.role: P5 + i5 primer for sgRNA library amplification (multiomic add-on)
oligos.notes: Printed with N5xx i5 placeholder and 3' phosphorothioates ...GAC*G*C. Used with Weissman_U6 to recover sgRNA from cDNA in CROP-seq.

oligos.name: Weissman_U6 (CROP-seq sgRNA enrichment)
oligos.role: P7 + i7 primer targeting the U6/guide scaffold for sgRNA capture
oligos.notes: Printed with N7xx i7 placeholder and 3' phosphorothioates ...TTGT*T*G. sgRNA library pooled 20:1 with RNA library before sequencing.

oligos.name: TotalSeq-A0253 anti-human Hashtag 3 (HTO)
oligos.notes: Captured on bead poly(T) via TotalSeq-A poly(A) handle; read as a 15-bp tag on Read 2 (trimmed to first 16 bp for CITE-seq Count).

oligos.name: TotalSeq-A0255 anti-human Hashtag 5 (HTO)
oligos.notes: See hto_a0253. A 19-plex TotalSeq-A ADT antibody panel (e.g. CD3 TATCCCTTGGGATGG, CD19 CTGGGCAATTACTCG, CD34 GCAGAAATCTCCCTT, CD14 TCTCAGACCTCCGTA, HLA-DR AATAGCGAGCAAGTA, plus CD64/CD22/CD10/CD13/CD117/CD5/CD7/CD56/CD11b/CD4/IgG1/CD30/CD33/CD45) is used for CITE-seq; these ADT barcodes are all 15-bp TotalSeq-A tags captured the same way.

final_library.source_label: PIPseq T20 3′ Single Cell RNA v4 (FB0002130) — dual-indexed Illumina 3′ gene-expression library
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: P5 | i5 | Read1_handle | CellBC(16) | UMI(12) | cDNA(3') | ligated_ME/Read2_handle | i7 | P7
annotation_lines: CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC       = ligated Nextera adapter / Read 2 handle
[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]                         = i7 sample index (reverse complement on top strand)

evidence.locator: PIPseq T20 3′ Single Cell RNA v4 (FB0002130) — dual-indexed Illumina 3′ gene-expression library

library_generation.title: Templated emulsification & cell capture
library_generation.summary: Cells are co-partitioned with barcoded T20 PIPs beads into monodisperse droplets by vortexing with Partitioning Reagent.
library_generation.note: 10 µL of ~4,000 cells/µL (40,000 cells) is mixed into a PIP bead tube, Partitioning Reagent is added, and the tube is vortexed 15 s horizontal + 2 min vertical at 3000 RPM. Each droplet ideally contains one cell and one barcoded bead.

library_generation.title: Lysis & mRNA capture on bead poly(T)
library_generation.summary: Chemical lysis emulsion + heat release mRNA, whose poly(A) tail anneals to the bead poly(T).
library_generation.note: 37 °C for cells (66 °C for nuclei). Released poly(A) mRNA hybridizes to the bead's poly(dT) anchor.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription with template switching
library_generation.summary: Maxima H-minus RT extends the bead primer into first-strand cDNA and template-switches onto the TSO.
library_generation.note: RT master mix (RT Additive Mix + TSO + RT Enzyme Mix): 25 °C 30 min, 42 °C 90 min, 85 °C 10 min. The TSO (AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAATGGG) is appended at the 3' end of the first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.product:
5'-...R1handle-[BC16]-[UMI12]-(dT)---> cDNA (3'gene) --->CCCATTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT-3'
                                                           ^template-switched TSO complement
  where R1handle = ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCC

library_generation.title: Whole-transcriptome amplification (WTA)
library_generation.summary: PCR with the WTA primer pair amplifies barcoded full-length cDNA off the two handles.
library_generation.note: WTA Buffer Mix + WTA Primer; 95 °C 3 min, ~11-16 cycles of 98 °C/69 °C/72 °C, 72 °C 5 min. Forward primer CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTC (Read1 handle) and reverse primer AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT (ISPCR/TSO handle).

library_generation.title: Fragmentation, end repair & A-tailing
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is enzymatically fragmented, end-repaired, and 3′ A-tailed.
library_generation.note: Library Prep Buffer + Enzymes; 30 °C 8 min (fragment) then 65 °C 30 min (end repair/A-tail). Generates blunt, A-tailed fragments enriched toward the 3' end/barcode end.

library_generation.title: Adapter ligation (Nextera-style forked adapter)
library_generation.summary: The Library Adapter Mix is ligated to A-tailed fragment ends, adding the Read 2 / P7-side handle.
library_generation.note: Library Adapter Mix (ligation adapter TATAAGAGACAGT / 5'P ligation primer CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCC) + Library Prep Mix A; 20 °C 15 min. The adapter's 3'-T pairs with the fragment A-overhang.

library_generation.title: Sample-index PCR (dual index) → final library
library_generation.summary: P5+i5 and P7+i7 primers add flow-cell adapters and sample indexes, then double-sided SPRI size selection.
library_generation.note: Library P5 Index + Library P7 Index + Library Prep Mix B; 98 °C 45 s, 6-12 cycles of 98/67/69, 72 °C 1 min. P5 primer reprimes the Read1 handle; P7 primer anneals to the ligated ME. Followed by 0.6x/0.8x double-sided size selection.

library_sequencing.note: Read 1 must be ≥54 nt. Sequencing primer anneals to the bottom strand at the Read 1 handle and reads the 16-nt cell barcode + 12-nt UMI, then runs into the poly(T).
8-nt i7 sample index. Index primer anneals to the bottom strand at the ligated Nextera ME / P7-side handle and reads the i7.

library_sequencing.primer: GGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT (reverse-complement of the Read 1 handle; used on reverse-complement-workflow instruments)
library_sequencing.note: 8-nt i5 sample index. On forward-strand instruments (MiSeq/NovaSeq fwd) the i5 is read directly off the Read 1 handle primer; on reverse-complement-workflow instruments (NextSeq/NovaSeq rev) the primer shown anneals to the top strand and reads the i5 as its complement.
Read 2 recommended ≥67 nt. Nextera Read 2 primer anneals to the top strand at the ligated ME/handle and reads back through the 3' end of the transcript (gene expression).
library_sequencing.diagram:
R2 primer 3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'  (anneals to top ME handle)
              <---------- reads cDNA gene body
5'-...[poly(T)][cDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:8]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
                     ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'-...[poly(A)][cDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:8]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'

title: PIP-seq (PIPseq T20 3′ v4)
description: PIPseq (Particle-templated Instant Partition sequencing) T20 3′ Single Cell RNA v4 is a microfluidics-free droplet scRNA-seq assay from Fluent BioSciences. Cells (or nuclei) are co-encapsulated with barcoded polyacrylamide hydrogel beads by vortex-driven templated emulsification in a Partitioning Reagent; heat + chemical lysis releases mRNA, which is captured on bead-bound poly(T) oligos carrying a cell barcode and a UMI. Reverse transcription with template switching (TSO) followed by whole-transcriptome amplification generates full-length cDNA, which is then fragmented, end-repaired/A-tailed, ligated to a Nextera-style adapter, and dual-indexed by PCR into a standard Illumina P5→P7 library. Read 1 captures the cell barcode + UMI; Read 2 captures the 3′ end of the transcript (gene expression).

reference.label: Microfluidics-free single-cell genomics with templated emulsification

original_publication.title: Microfluidics-free single-cell genomics with templated emulsification

throughput.summary: v4 T20 kit: up to 80,000 cells/nuclei per kit (4 reactions x ~20,000 recovered); ~40,000 cells loaded per reaction; multiplet rate <8%. Paper demonstrated ~10 to ~1,000,000 cells in a single tube by scaling container volume.
throughput.cells: 20,000 recovered per reaction (80,000/kit); up to ~1e6 in a 50-mL single-tube format
throughput.rna: 3' gene expression; recommended ~20,000 reads per input cell
throughput.dna: Illumina paired-end library, P5->P7, dual 8-bp i5/i7 indexes

publication.statistical_model: Poisson single-cell/single-bead co-encapsulation; cell calling via an in-house emptyDrops implementation (per-barcode P-value against an ambient expression profile, Benjamini-Hochberg-adjusted P<0.001); UMI-based transcript counting.

other.label: Vendor / kit
other.value: Fluent BioSciences, PIPseq T20 3' Single Cell RNA Kit v4.0 (Doc FB0002130)

other.label: Instrument-free partitioning
other.value: Vortex-driven templated emulsification in the Partitioning Reagent — no microfluidics; 96/384/1,536-well plate compatible

other.label: Verified sequencers
other.value: Illumina MiSeq, NextSeq 550, NextSeq 2000, NovaSeq 6000; >=1% PhiX; R1>=54, R2>=67

other.label: Multiomic support
other.value: CITE-seq (TotalSeq-A ADTs), cell hashing (HTOs), and CROP-seq sgRNA capture (P5-PE1 + Weissman_U6)

other.label: Data / analysis
other.value: GEO SuperSeries GSE202919; analyzed with Salmon alevin and PIPseeker (v2.x for v4 chemistry)

data_processing.summary: Custom Fluent BioSciences pipeline over the FASTQs: demultiplex, error-correct the cell barcode against a known whitelist (Hamming distance ≤1), pseudo-align reads to the full transcriptome with Salmon alevin, emit a UMI count matrix, then separate real cells from ambient with an in-house Python emptyDrops implementation before normalization, Seurat clustering and UMAP. PIPseeker is the productized (STAR-based) equivalent; CITE-seq-Count handles the ADT/HTO tags in the multiomic add-ons.
data_processing.statistical_model: emptyDrops ambient-profile test (Benjamini–Hochberg-adjusted P<0.001) for cell calling; Poisson single-bead/single-cell co-encapsulation; UMI-based transcript counting.
